title: Deterministic code rules for AI-generated code
shortDescription: A ruleset for steering AI-generated code towards simplicity, stability, and determinism
context: AI-generated code tends to be inconsistent, implicit, and hard to maintain.
problem: Code from LLMs often contains heuristics, hidden dependencies, and poor error handling.
approach: Define a strict ruleset that constrains how code may be written and structured.

systemDesign.processing: Rule enforcement via explicit constraints and structural requirements
systemDesign.output: Code that is deterministic, explicit, and easy to verify
systemDesign.constraints: No implicit logic, no silent failures, full traceability

result: More stable and predictable code from AI generation
notes: The ruleset is actively used to govern AI-generated code in personal projects.
designPrinciple: Everything that can be made explicit must be made explicit

title: AI-based job matching and skills analysis
shortDescription: Job matching based on AI-extracted skills and trend analysis
context: Job listings often lack structure, making it hard to match skills manually.
problem: Difficult to assess relevance between a CV and job listings, or identify demanded skills.
approach: Extract skills from both CV and job listings using AI, then calculate matching based on that.

systemDesign.input: CV + job listings from the AF API
systemDesign.processing: AI extraction → normalisation → matching algorithm → trend analysis
systemDesign.output: Match score per job + skill trends + generated cover letter
systemDesign.constraints: Job listings lack structure and require robust extraction

tech: AF API, LLM for extraction/generation, local storage for trend data
result: Produces match scores, identifies relevant jobs, and surfaces changes in demanded skills
notes: Trend analysis is based on local history, giving a limited but fast signal.
designPrinciple: Structured skill data is prioritised over raw text to enable comparability

title: Code agent: limitations of AI-driven code generation
shortDescription: Analysis of why autonomous code agents are hard to make stable and production-ready
context: An attempt to build an agent that can independently plan, write, and modify code via an iterative agent loop.
problem: AI-generated code is hard to make stable, verifiable, and consistent across iterations, especially in systems with autonomous execution.
approach: Experimented with agent loops, tool-calling, code generation, and various forms of validation and feedback.

systemDesign.processing: Planning → code generation → tool calls → execution → validation → iteration
systemDesign.constraints: Hard to guarantee determinism, correctness, and consistent behaviour across iterations in an autonomous system

tech: LLM, agent loops, tool-calling, code execution
result: Identifies where autonomous code agents break down and what architectural and verification constraints are needed to build stable systems.
notes: The project was paused after the realisation that the problem requires stricter control, clearer constraints, and more verifiable architecture than a fully autonomous system allows.
designPrinciple: Limited autonomy with a focus on control, determinism, and verifiability over maximum independence

title: Structured CV generation with AI
shortDescription: Form-based CV generation with AI support and ATS-optimised output
context: Many CVs lack clear structure and are hard to adapt manually for different jobs.
problem: Unstructured CVs perform worse in ATS systems and require a lot of manual editing.
approach: Separate structure from content. Use forms for structure and AI for extraction and improvement.

systemDesign.input: Form data or an existing CV
systemDesign.processing: AI extraction → structuring → improvement suggestions → preview rendering
systemDesign.constraints: Must be consistently structured and minimise free text

tech: Form-based UI, LLM for extraction/improvement, HTML rendering
result: Fast generation of structured CVs with reduced manual iteration
notes: AI extraction requires normalisation for stable output. Preview function simplifies iteration.
designPrinciple: Structure is prioritised over flexibility to ensure ATS compatibility
